note: Phase 2 crate sweep (PLAYBOOK-TERRAFORM-VIBEVM-v0.2 #phase2; PROP-014 §2.7 LLM-as-proposer). Proposals only — no source file edited. All targeted spec units except the pilot five are UNMARKED (no kind line); every proposed edge is therefore UNPINNED (no `r`) so the affirmation does not multiply pin-into-unmarked-unit warnings — pins arrive when the units get unit-ified. conditional.rs is excluded (tagged by the Phase 1 pilot). #req-conditional-fixpoint deliberately receives no proposal from this crate: the re-solve loop lives in workspace install orchestration, not vibe-resolver (PHASE1-PILOT.md §1 judgment call 2). Mined commit b794e7a (PROP-007 manifest unification) yields no edge: vibe-resolver only consumes vibe-core::Manifest types there — borrowing a type is not implementing the unit. Two reader notes: (1) ResolvedNode's doc-comment cites 'PROP-008 §2.3' while the identity-tuple anchor is #identity (§2.2) — §2.3 is #kind ('kind is pure metadata', the comment's second clause), so the comment is half-right on both; proposal targets #identity by content. (2) features.rs `expand_features` doc says 'Cycles are detected and rejected' but the implementation (and test cycles_terminate) terminates cycles silently via a seen-set — doc-string drift inside the crate, flagged for the affirmation pass to fix or not; no edge proposed on it.

mined_commits.subject: feat(resolver): the PROP-014 pilot — PROP-003 units x conditional.rs tags
feat(core): mandatory [package].group
mined_commits.disposition: group-qualification of solver-internal refs; supports PRP-0001/PRP-0004 evidence

mined_commits.subject: feat(core): unify manifests into vibe.toml (PROP-007)
mined_commits.disposition: no edge — type consumption only (see note)

proposals.evidence_code: Reverse-FQDN group — half of the `(group, name)` identity tuple (PROP-008 §2.3). `kind` is pure metadata and is not carried here.
proposals.evidence_spec: Package identity becomes `(group, name, version, content_hash)`. `kind` **leaves the identity tuple**.

proposals.evidence_code: Direct dependencies of this node, pinned to exact versions chosen by the solver. Lockfile `dependencies` field is built from this.
proposals.evidence_spec: `dependencies` field per package is **resolved** (exact version, not constraint) — the lockfile is the full resolved graph, not a constraint manifest.

proposals.evidence_code: The full resolved graph for one solver invocation.
proposals.evidence_spec: Both implement the same `crates/vibe-resolver/src/lib.rs::DepSolver` trait (`fn solve(&self, roots: &[PackageRef]) -> Result<ResolvedGraph, SolveError>`).

proposals.evidence_code: Find a node by `(group, name)` identity.
proposals.evidence_spec: `name` becomes unique **within a `group`** […] `(group, name)` is therefore unique on its own — `kind` is no longer needed to disambiguate.

proposals.evidence_code: Implementors fan out to multi-registry / mirror / override resolution as needed; the solver treats this as a black box.
proposals.evidence_spec: Provider trait (`DependencyProvider` analog) maps cleanly onto our existing `Registry` / `MultiRegistryResolver` types; no FFI, no impedance mismatch, no C-toolchain dependency.

proposals.evidence_code: What the install / update pipeline calls. […] fn solve(&self, roots: &[PackageRef]) -> Result<ResolvedGraph, SolveError>
proposals.evidence_spec: A `DepSolver` trait in the new `vibe-resolver` crate mirrors the PROP-001 §2.2 `GitBackend` pattern: primary impl is `ResolvoSolver`; a future `LibsolvSolver` […] drops in as a feature-gated alternative.

proposals.reason: §2.1 specifies the trait gains `pin_preferences(&mut self, pins)` for minimum-churn re-resolution; the method is absent — PROP-011 Phase 3 holds pins via constraint-tightening at the install layer instead, and SatDepSolver is not in tree (debt DBT-0011 adjacent).
proposals.evidence_code: pub trait DepSolver { fn solve(&self, roots: &[PackageRef]) -> Result<ResolvedGraph, SolveError>; } — no `pin_preferences` method exists.
proposals.evidence_spec: **`DepSolver` trait — minimal additions.** The trait gains one method: […] fn pin_preferences(&mut self, _pins: &[(PackageRef, semver::Version)]) {}

proposals.evidence_code: package `{group}/{name}` is not available in any configured registry […] Aggregate-walk variant of `UnknownPackage` — same "not available" verdict, but with structured per-registry `attempts`.
proposals.evidence_spec: The priority-ordered registry walk falls through on **`UnknownPackage` only**: a registry that confidently answers "I don't have this package" is free to defer to the next one.

proposals.evidence_code: capability `{capability}` required by `{requirer}` is not provided by any package in the resolved graph. […] all alternatives in `[[requires_any]]` declared by `{requirer}` failed to resolve.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[requires].capabilities = ["<namespace>:<name>[@<constraint>]", …]` — satisfied by any package that provides that capability.

proposals.evidence_code: DFS solver over a [`DepProvider`]. [module doc:] Single pass, no backtracking.
proposals.evidence_spec: `NaiveDepSolver` stays in tree as the "small graphs / no features / no disjunctions" fast path; `SatDepSolver` becomes the default once the second-tier features in this PROP land.

proposals.reason: §2.8 decides resolvo is the PRIMARY depsolver; no ResolvoSolver exists in tree — NaiveDepSolver is the only DepSolver impl. Known debt (DBT-0011, SAT/resolvo upgrade); the deviation is the honest record until the second impl lands.
proposals.evidence_code: When any of these limits start hurting real users […] that is the trigger for adding a `ResolvoSolver` (PROP-002 §2.8 primary).
proposals.evidence_spec: **Decision.** The primary depsolver is the [`resolvo`](https://crates.io/crates/resolvo) crate (pure Rust, BSD-3-Clause-or-Apache-2.0, used by Pixi and Rattler at conda scale).

proposals.evidence_code: Pin every dependency reference to the exact version chosen for it in the graph. Lockfile `dependencies` per entry stores exact-pinned `group/name@=version`.
proposals.evidence_spec: `dependencies` field per package is **resolved** (exact version, not constraint) — the lockfile is the full resolved graph, not a constraint manifest.

proposals.evidence_code: Build the output graph. Order: roots first (input order preserved), then the rest sorted for determinism.
proposals.evidence_spec: Two solves with the same inputs produce the same result. This is a property we explicitly rely on for `cargo xtask check-codegen`-style drift checks.

proposals.evidence_code: Order matters: org.vibevm/rust provides ui:landing-page, then org.vibevm/home requires it. Naive provider-then-consumer ordering succeeds.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[requires].capabilities = ["<namespace>:<name>[@<constraint>]", …]` — satisfied by any package that provides that capability.

proposals.evidence_code: A package that both provides and requires the same capability with the same exact version trivially satisfies itself.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[provides].capabilities = ["<namespace>:<name>[@<semver>]", …]` — abstract capabilities the package advertises.

proposals.evidence_code: SolveError::CapabilityUnmet { capability, .. } => { assert!(capability.contains("ui:landing-page")); }
proposals.evidence_spec: `[requires].capabilities` […] — satisfied by any package that provides that capability.

proposals.evidence_code: org.vibevm/ui declares conflicts with org.vibevm/legacy-wal; if both are roots, solver refuses.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[conflicts].packages = [ pkgrefs… ]` — mutually exclusive installs.

proposals.evidence_code: After solve, legacy is removed via obsoletes.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[obsoletes].packages = [ pkgrefs… ]` — the package supersedes these; the solver flags them for removal on upgrade.

proposals.evidence_code: org.vibevm/x requires_any [org.vibevm/a, org.vibevm/b]; only org.vibevm/a available. […] First alternative gets enqueued; resolution succeeds.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[[requires_any]] one_of = [ pkgrefs… ]` — disjunction; exactly one must be satisfied. Repeatable table for multiple independent disjunctions.

proposals.evidence_code: Two roots: one wants ^0.1, the other wants ^0.2. […] the second's constraint is checked against it — fails because 0.2.0 doesn't match ^0.1.
proposals.evidence_spec: Semantic: the solver computes a satisfying assignment over the declared constraints.

proposals.evidence_code: Dep must be pinned to the exact version chosen, not the original `^0.1` constraint. A future re-install reads this pin verbatim to reproduce the same graph.
proposals.evidence_spec: `dependencies` field per package is **resolved** (exact version, not constraint) — the lockfile is the full resolved graph, not a constraint manifest.

proposals.evidence_code: let roots: Vec<_> = graph.roots().collect(); assert_eq!(roots.len(), 1); assert_eq!(roots[0].name, "wal");
proposals.evidence_spec: `root_dependencies` is a **mirror of `vibe.toml` `[requires].packages`** — the lockfile keeps the user's declared roots inline so it remains a self-contained snapshot of the solve state.

proposals.evidence_code: SolveError::Provider(crate::DepProviderError::UnknownPackage { .. }) => {}
proposals.evidence_spec: The priority-ordered registry walk falls through on **`UnknownPackage` only**: a registry that confidently answers "I don't have this package" is free to defer to the next one.

proposals.evidence_code: `foo/feat` (strong) or `foo?/feat` (weak). […] `subskill:<path>` — activates a subskill.
proposals.evidence_spec: **Weak feature** (cargo's `dep?/feat` syntax): `feat-A = ["other-pkg?/some-feat"]` — if `other-pkg` is *already* in the graph, request `some-feat` on it; otherwise no-op.

proposals.evidence_code: Merge another expansion into this one. Used at the cross-package unification layer.
proposals.evidence_spec: **Feature unification across the dep graph.** If `pkg-A` and `pkg-B` both depend on `pkg-C` and request different features, the solver unifies — `pkg-C` is built/materialised once with the union of requested features.

proposals.evidence_code: `--no-default-features` — skip the `default` feature's activation. […] `--all-features` — activate every named feature in the table.
proposals.evidence_spec: **Default features.** `default = [...]` lists features active when no override is given. `--no-default-features` on the install / update CLI omits them.

proposals.evidence_code: Expand a `[features]` table from the requested starting set. […] Exclusive-group violations flag as `ExclusiveViolation`.
proposals.evidence_spec: **Feature-feature dependency.** `feat-A = ["feat-B"]` — enabling A enables B transitively.

proposals.evidence_code: Static structural validation of a `[features]` table — runs at `vibe check` time. Returns a list of diagnostics (empty = valid).
proposals.evidence_spec: (Cargo enforces this informally; vibevm enforces it via `vibe check` since spec content collisions are easier to detect than code-level ones.)

proposals.evidence_code: private feature `{0}` cannot be activated by name […] exclusive group `{group}` violated — multiple features active: {active:?}
proposals.evidence_spec: **Feature visibility.** Features prefixed with `_` (underscore) are *implementation details* — invisible to consumer manifests; cannot be activated by name from outside the package.

proposals.evidence_code: FeatureValue::parse("baz?/qux").unwrap() == FeatureValue::DepFeature { dep_name: "baz", dep_feature: "qux", weak: true }
proposals.evidence_spec: **Weak feature** (cargo's `dep?/feat` syntax) […] The `?` prevents activation-by-default of `other-pkg`.

proposals.evidence_code: assert!(exp.active_features.contains("wal-protocol")); assert!(!exp.active_features.contains("optional-x"));
proposals.evidence_spec: **Default features.** `default = [...]` lists features active when no override is given.

proposals.evidence_code: no_defaults: true […] assert!(!exp.active_features.contains("default"));
proposals.evidence_spec: `--no-default-features` on the install / update CLI omits them.

proposals.evidence_code: assert!(exp.active_features.contains("public-a")); assert!(!exp.active_features.contains("_internal-helper"));
proposals.evidence_spec: Features prefixed with `_` (underscore) are *implementation details* — invisible to consumer manifests; cannot be activated by name from outside the package.

proposals.evidence_code: assert!(matches!(err, FeatureError::PrivateFeature(_)));
proposals.evidence_spec: Cargo has an informal convention here; we make it solver-enforced.

proposals.evidence_code: default = ["a"], a = ["b", "c"], b = ["dep:bar"], c = ["d/feat", "subskill:stack/rust"] […] assert!(exp.active_subskills.contains("stack/rust"));
proposals.evidence_spec: **Feature → subskill mapping.** A feature can list `subskill:<path>` in its activation list, which directs the resolver to materialise the corresponding subskill (§2.5).

proposals.evidence_code: assert!(!exp.active_deps.contains("other")); […] weak_dep_features.get("other").unwrap().contains("some-feat")
proposals.evidence_spec: if `other-pkg` is *already* in the graph, request `some-feat` on it; otherwise no-op. The `?` prevents activation-by-default of `other-pkg`.

proposals.evidence_code: [exclusive] stacks = ["rust-stack", "python-stack"] […] FeatureError::ExclusiveViolation { group, active } => { assert_eq!(group, "stacks");
proposals.evidence_spec: **Mutual exclusion** via `[features.exclusive]` named groups — each value list is an at-most-one set.

proposals.evidence_code: a.merge(&b); assert!(a.active_features.contains("foo")); assert!(a.active_features.contains("baz"));
proposals.evidence_spec: **Feature unification across the dep graph.** […] the solver unifies — `pkg-C` is built/materialised once with the union of requested features.

proposals.evidence_code: Capabilities and pkgrefs present in the resolved graph. Examples: `flow:wal`, `stack:rust`, `capability:wal-protocol`.
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_present`.** Activates if the project's effective dep graph contains a named package or capability (`stack:rust`, `capability:wal-protocol`).

proposals.evidence_code: Outcome of evaluating one subskill's activation rules. `Active` carries the channels that fired (for diagnostic output).
proposals.evidence_spec: Channels compose orthogonally; an active subskill activates once regardless of how many matched.

proposals.evidence_code: Channel semantics: ANY match activates. An empty rule block (no probes specified) returns `active = false` — manual activation via parent feature is handled at a higher layer.
proposals.evidence_spec: `[activation]` with no probes at all means the subskill activates only manually (via parent feature).

proposals.reason: §2.5.2 lists `if_os` among the channels, 'reserved in the schema […] inert until the activation engine is built'. The engine now exists (this fn) and still does not evaluate if_os. Arguably as-designed (the spec marks it reserved); owner call whether this is a deviation worth recording or the spec sentence already covers it.
proposals.evidence_code: evaluate() probes if_present / if_provides / if_files / if_command / if_env / if_describes_match / if_language — no `if_os` branch.
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_os`.** […] On the subskill side it is reserved in the schema for forward compatibility, inert until the activation engine is built.

proposals.evidence_code: let rules = ActivationRules::default(); […] assert!(!outcome.active);
proposals.evidence_spec: `[activation]` with no probes at all means the subskill activates only manually (via parent feature).

proposals.evidence_code: ctx.add_present("stack:rust"); […] assert_eq!(outcome.channels_matched, vec!["if_present"]);
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_present`.** Activates if the project's effective dep graph contains a named package or capability (`stack:rust`, `capability:wal-protocol`).

proposals.evidence_code: ctx.add_provides("interface:build-system"); […] assert_eq!(outcome.channels_matched, vec!["if_provides"]);
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_provides`.** Activates if any package in the graph declares `[provides]` matching the named interface tag (`interface:build-system`).

proposals.evidence_code: if_files: vec!["**/Cargo.toml".into()] […] assert_eq!(outcome.channels_matched, vec!["if_files"]);
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_files`.** New in r2. Activates if the project tree matches one of the supplied glob patterns (`**/Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `requirements.txt`).

proposals.evidence_code: ctx.describes_types.insert("cargo".into()); let outcome = evaluate(&rules, &ctx, Some("cargo")); assert!(outcome.active);
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_describes_match`.** New in r2. Activates if any package in the graph […] declares `describes` with a PURL whose `type` matches this subskill's `describes` type.

proposals.evidence_code: language_chain: vec!["ru".into(), "en".into()] […] assert!(outcome.active);
proposals.evidence_spec: **Context-based: `if_language`.** Activates if the consumer's resolved language preference (§2.7.3) is in the listed set. Carried over from r1.

proposals.evidence_code: assert!(outcome.active); assert_eq!(outcome.channels_matched.len(), 2);
proposals.evidence_spec: Channels compose orthogonally; an active subskill activates once regardless of how many matched.

proposals.evidence_code: `DepProvider` adapter over a [`vibe_registry::LocalRegistry`]. For the `--registry <path>` install path.
proposals.evidence_spec: Provider trait (`DependencyProvider` analog) maps cleanly onto our existing `Registry` / `MultiRegistryResolver` types.

proposals.evidence_code: Wraps the multi-registry / mirror / override dispatch from [`vibe_registry::MultiRegistryResolver`] in the trait the solver consumes.
proposals.evidence_spec: Provider trait (`DependencyProvider` analog) maps cleanly onto our existing `Registry` / `MultiRegistryResolver` types.

proposals.evidence_code: Delegate to the resolver's redirect-aware `fetch_manifest`: walks registries in priority order, follows any `vibe-redirect.toml` stub it lands on (PROP-002 §2.4.2), returns the target's manifest.
proposals.evidence_spec: The resolver, when fetching the package manifest, transparently follows the pointer; consumers `vibe install <pkgref>` see no difference from a direct registry-resolved package.

proposals.evidence_code: Preserve the structured per-registry attempts so the downstream install-error JSON envelope can ship them verbatim.
proposals.evidence_spec: When a primary `[[registry]]` connect-fails, the error message points the operator at `vibe registry list` (typo check) and the network (outage check).
